untranslatable_reason: Signal requires pattern recognition (two bottoms, highest high between bottoms, lowest low identification) which cannot be expressed with allowed indicators and operators. The measure rule target calculation (Highest high + (Highest high - Lower bottom)) requires custom pattern detection logic not available in indicator-based rules. Volume comparison between left and right bottoms also requires explicit pattern detection.
translation_notes: This is a classic chart pattern signal (double bottom/two bottoms) that fundamentally requires candlestick pattern detection rather than technical indicator thresholds. The entry is context-dependent on identifying the specific pattern structure. To implement this signal, a custom pattern scanner would be needed to: (1) identify two distinct bottoms, (2) find the highest high between them, (3) detect breakout above that high, and (4) calculate the target using the provided measure rule formula. The 66% success rate in bull markets and 48% in bear markets should be stored in metadata but cannot be encoded as executable rules.
